sections.heading: Definition
sections.body:
The [$lookup](streams-agg-pipeline-lookup) stage performs a left outer
join of the stream of messages from your [$source](pipe.$source) to an
Atlas collection in your [Connection Registry](atlas-sp-manage-connections).

Depending on your use case, a `$lookup` pipeline stage uses one of
the following three syntaxes:

- [Equality Match with a Single Join Collection](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup)

- [Join Conditions and Subqueries on a Joined Collection](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup)

- [Correlated Subqueries Using Concise Syntax](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup)

To learn more, see [$lookup Syntax](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup).

> **Warning:** Using `$lookup` to enrich a stream can reduce stream processing
> speed.

The following prototype form illustrates all available fields:

```json
{
  "$lookup": {
    "from": {
      "connectionName": "<registered-atlas-connection>",
      "db": "<registered-database-name>",
      "coll": "<atlas-collection-name>"
    },
    "localField": "<field-in-source-messages>",
    "foreignField": "<field-in-from-collection>",
    "let": {
      <var_1>: <expression>,
      <var_2>: <expression>,
      …,
      <var_n>: <expression>
    },
    "pipeline": [
      <pipeline to run>
    ],
    "as": "<output-array-field>",
    "parallelism": <integer>,
    "partitionBy": <expression>
  }
}
```

sections.heading: Syntax
sections.body:
The `$lookup` stage takes a document with the following fields:

| Field               | Type       | Necessity   | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------- | ---------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| from                | document   | Conditional | Document that specifies a collection in an Atlas database to join to messages from your $source. You must specify a collection only from your Connection Registry.If you specify this field, you must specify values for all fields in this document.This field is not required if you specify a pipeline field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| from.connectionName | string     | Conditional | Name of the connection in your Connection Registry.This field is not required if you specify a pipeline field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| from.db             | string     | Conditional | Name of the Atlas database that contains the collection you want to join.This field is not required if you specify a pipeline field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| from.coll           | string     | Conditional | Name of the collection you want to join.This field is not required if you specify a pipeline field.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| localField          | string     | Conditional | Field from your $source messages to join on.This field is part of the following syntaxes:Equality Match with a Single Join CollectionCorrelated Subqueries Using Concise Syntax                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| foreignField        | string     | Conditional | Field from documents in the from collection to join on.This field is part of the following syntaxes:Equality Match with a Single Join CollectionCorrelated Subqueries Using Concise Syntax                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| let                 | document   | Conditional | Specifies the variables to use in the pipeline stages. To learn more, see let.This field is part of the following syntaxes:Join Conditions and Subqueries on a Joined CollectionCorrelated Subqueries Using Concise Syntax                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| pipeline            | document   | Conditional | Specifies the pipeline to run on the joined collection. To learn more, see pipeline.This field is part of the following syntaxes:Join Conditions and Subqueries on a Joined CollectionCorrelated Subqueries Using Concise Syntax                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| as                  | string     | Required    | Name of the new array field to add to the input documents. This new array field contains the matching documents from the from collection. If the specified name already exists as a field in the input document, that field is overwritten.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| parallelism         | integer    | Optional    | Maximum number of parallel requests made to the $lookup target. Higher parallelism values may increase $lookup throughput. However, higher values may also use more resources on the $lookup target cluster.Must be an integer between 1 and 16. Defaults to 1.Each stream processor has a maximum cumulative parallelism value determined by its tier. The cumulative parallelism of a stream processor is calculated as follows:parallelism total - parallelized stagesWhere parallelism total is the sum of all parallelism values greater than 1 across the $source, $lookup, and $merge stages, and parallelized stages is the number of these stages with parallelism values greater than 1.For example, if your $source stage sets a parallelism value of 4, your $lookup stage sets no parallelism value (thus defaulting to 1), and your $merge stage sets a parallelism value of 2, then you have two parallelized stages, and the cumulative parallelism of your stream processor is calculated as (4 + 2) - 2.If a stream processor exceeds the maximum cumulative parallelism for its tier, Atlas Stream Processing throws an error and advises you of the minimum processor tier required for your intended level of parallelism. You must either scale the processor up to a higher tier or lower the parallelism values of your stages to resolve the error. To learn more, see Stream Processing. |
| partitionBy         | expression | Optional    | Expression used to partition the input stream into parallel threads. Atlas Stream Processing allocates each input document with the same partitionBy expression result to the same thread.If this field is unspecified, input documents are sent to parallel threads by round-robin.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

sections.heading: Behavior
sections.body:
The Atlas Stream Processing version of [$lookup](streams-agg-pipeline-lookup)
performs a left outer join of messages from your `$source` and the
documents in a specified Atlas collection. This version behaves
similarly to the [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) stage available in a standard
MongoDB database. However, this version requires that you specify an
Atlas collection from your
[Connection Registry](atlas-sp-manage-connections) as the value for
the `from` field.

The [pipeline](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) can contain a nested
[$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) stage. If you include a nested [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup)
stage in your pipeline, you must use the standard `from` syntax to
specify a collection in the same remote Atlas connection as the
outer [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) stage.

> **Example:** 
>
> ```json
> $lookup : {
>   from: {connectionName: "dbsrv1", db: "db1", coll: "coll1"},
>     …,
>   pipeline: [
>     …,
>     {
>       $lookup: {
>         from: "coll2",
>         …,
>       }
>     },
>     …,
>   ]
> }
> ```

If your pipeline has both [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) and [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge)
on the same collection, Atlas Stream Processing results might vary if you
try to maintain an incremental view. Atlas Stream Processing processes
multiple source messages simultaneously and then merges them all
together. If multiple messages have the same ID, which both
[$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) and [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) use, Atlas Stream Processing might
return results that haven't yet materialized.

> **Example:** Consider the following input stream:
>
> ```shell
> { _id: 1, count: 2 }
> { _id: 1, count: 3 }
> ```
>
> Suppose your query contains the following inside the pipeline:
>
> ```js
> {
>   ...,
>   pipeline: [
>     { $lookup on _id == foreignDoc._id from collection A }
>     { $project: { _id: 1, count: $count + $foreignDoc.count } }
>     { $merge: { into collection A } }
>   ]
> }
> ```
>
> If you are trying to maintain an incremental view, you might expect a
> result similar to the following:
>
> ```shell
> { _id: 1, count: 5 }
> ```
>
> However, Atlas Stream Processing might return a count of `5` or `3`
> depending on whether Atlas Stream Processing has processed the documents.

For more information, see [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup).

sections.heading: Examples
